description_long: This object is a vintage-style gas pump with a weathered, rusted exterior, predominantly painted in teal and blue with patches of exposed metal and rust. The front features a rectangular display window with a black panel showing fuel totals and gallons, as well as a round amber indicator light above it. A metallic fuel nozzle and hose are mounted on the right side, with the hose hanging down and looping to the back. The rear side is mostly plain, with a large teal panel and visible rust, while the left and right sides show additional metallic details and mounting points for the hose and nozzle. The pump stands on a rectangular concrete or stone base, giving it a sturdy, immovable appearance. The overall design and patina suggest it is an older, possibly mid-20th-century model, suitable for a retro or abandoned urban environment.
description: A weathered, vintage gas pump with teal and blue paint, rust patches, a display window, and a metallic fuel nozzle on a concrete base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the display window, amber indicator light, and the fuel nozzle mounted on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlights the blue back panel, rust patches, and the hose and nozzle hanging down the back.
description_view_2: Side view reveals a large teal panel with significant rust and the side profile of the nozzle and hose.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays a blue panel with a circular metallic component and a small rectangular plate, with no nozzle visible.
attribute_fuel_pumps: One vintage-style fuel pump is present, featuring a single metallic nozzle and black rubber hose mounted on the right side. The pump is painted teal and blue with significant rust, and has a rectangular display window and an amber indicator light. No visible branding is present.
